data.description:
The completion-verification close gate (_completion_precheck in src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py) runs full LLM completion verification on EVERY non---force-close bug close, with NO awareness that some closes are legitimately NON-completion:

- close_class handling (transition_close.py ~line 124): only validates that --class is a VALID bug-close value (bug_close_class_ok); it does NOT exempt the classes that mean 'there is no completion to verify' — duplicate, not_a_bug, escalated.
- criteria scope (~line 172, graph=False): verifies only THIS ticket's OWN acceptance criteria; it does not consult the ticket's dependency graph for a supersedes/duplicates link. A bug that another ticket SUPERSEDES (its work has legitimately moved to the superseding ticket/epic) is still forced to prove its OWN completion, which by definition cannot pass.

REPRO: bug e983-4df0-0d53-4811 is superseded by epic 992d-9d17-c6ac-4492 (a 'supersedes' link exists).  -> 'completion verification FAILED — 1 unmet criteria; not closing.' The only ways past are --force-close (a gate BYPASS) or the idea->closed exemption (a hack) — neither is a legitimate 'close as superseded'.

EXPECTED: a close whose --class is duplicate/not_a_bug/escalated, OR whose ticket carries a supersedes/duplicates link to an open/valid ticket, should SKIP completion verification (there is no own-completion to verify) while still recording the class + link. Same class of gap as ed13/0e15 (a lifecycle requirement not propagated to a branch that should be exempt).

Discovered while superseding e983 into epic 992d during the rebar-debug batch.
data.title: Completion-verification close gate ignores superseding/duplicate relationships and non-completion --class values
